ammonyte.tests package¶

Submodules¶

ammonyte.tests.conftest module¶

Shared test fixtures for ammonyte testing

This module contains shared pytest fixtures for synthetic data generation. Following best practices for scientific Python packages like Pyleoclim.

ammonyte.tests.conftest.gen_series_with_transitions()[source]¶

Generate time series with artificial transitions at 30% and 70% positions

ammonyte.tests.conftest.gen_smooth_series()[source]¶

Generate smooth sinusoidal series without transitions

ammonyte.tests.test_core_recurrence_matrix module¶

Tests for ammonyte.core.recurrence_matrix Naming rules: 1. class: Test{filename}{Class}{method} with appropriate camel case 2. function: test_{method}_t{test_id}

Notes on how to test: 0. Make sure [pytest](https://docs.pytest.org) has been installed: pip install pytest 1. execute pytest {directory_path} in terminal to perform all tests in all testing files inside the specified directory

(certain tests will only work when run from the tests directory, so make sure to run from there!)

  1. execute pytest {file_path} in terminal to perform all tests in the specified file

  2. execute pytest {file_path}::{TestClass}::{test_method} in terminal to perform a specific test class/method inside the specified file

  3. after pip install pytest-xdist, one may execute “pytest -n 4” to test in parallel with number of workers specified by -n

  4. for more details, see https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/usage.html

class ammonyte.tests.test_core_recurrence_matrix.TestCoreRecurrenceMatrixLaplacianEigenmaps[source]¶

Bases: object

Tests for laplacian eigenmaps function

test_laplacian_eigenmaps_t0()[source]¶
ammonyte.tests.test_core_recurrence_matrix.gen_normal(loc=0, scale=1, nt=100)[source]¶

Generate random data with a Gaussian distribution

ammonyte.tests.test_core_recurrence_network module¶

Tests for ammonyte.core.recurrence_network Naming rules: 1. class: Test{filename}{Class}{method} with appropriate camel case 2. function: test_{method}_t{test_id}

Notes on how to test: 0. Make sure [pytest](https://docs.pytest.org) has been installed: pip install pytest 1. execute pytest {directory_path} in terminal to perform all tests in all testing files inside the specified directory

(certain tests will only work when run from the tests directory, so make sure to run from there!)

  1. execute pytest {file_path} in terminal to perform all tests in the specified file

  2. execute pytest {file_path}::{TestClass}::{test_method} in terminal to perform a specific test class/method inside the specified file

  3. after pip install pytest-xdist, one may execute “pytest -n 4” to test in parallel with number of workers specified by -n

  4. for more details, see https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/usage.html

  3. after pip install pytest-xdist, one may execute “pytest -n 4” to test in parallel with number of workers specified by -n

Tests for ammonyte.core.series Naming rules: 1. class: Test{filename}{Class}{method} with appropriate camel case 2. function: test_{method}_t{test_id}

Notes on how to test: 0. Make sure [pytest](https://docs.pytest.org) has been installed: pip install pytest 1. execute pytest {directory_path} in terminal to perform all tests in all testing files inside the specified directory

(certain tests will only work when run from the tests directory, so make sure to run from there!)

  1. execute pytest {file_path} in terminal to perform all tests in the specified file

  2. execute pytest {file_path}::{TestClass}::{test_method} in terminal to perform a specific test class/method inside the specified file

  3. after pip install pytest-xdist, one may execute “pytest -n 4” to test in parallel with number of workers specified by -n

  4. for more details, see https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/usage.html

(certain tests will only work when run from the tests directory, so make sure to run from there!)

  2. execute pytest {file_path}::{TestClass}::{test_method} in terminal to perform a specific test class/method inside the specified file

  3. after pip install pytest-xdist, one may execute “pytest -n 4” to test in parallel with number of workers specified by -n

ammonyte.tests.test_utils_ks module¶

Tests for ammonyte.utils.ks Naming rules: 1. class: Test{filename}{Class}{method} with appropriate camel case 2. function: test_{method}_t{test_id}

Notes on how to test: 0. Make sure [pytest](https://docs.pytest.org) has been installed: pip install pytest 1. execute pytest {directory_path} in terminal to perform all tests in all testing files inside the specified directory

(certain tests will only work when run from the tests directory, so make sure to run from there!)

  1. execute pytest {file_path} in terminal to perform all tests in the specified file

  2. execute pytest {file_path}::{TestClass}::{test_method} in terminal to perform a specific test class/method inside the specified file

  3. after pip install pytest-xdist, one may execute “pytest -n 4” to test in parallel with number of workers specified by -n

  4. for more details, see https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/usage.html

class ammonyte.tests.test_utils_ks.TestUtilsKsBasic[source]¶

Bases: object

Essential tests for KS_test function

test_ks_function_exists_t0()[source]¶

Test that KS_test function can be imported

test_ks_return_types_t0(gen_series_with_transitions)[source]¶

Test KS_test returns correct data types and structures

test_ks_value_ranges_t0(gen_series_with_transitions)[source]¶

Test returned values are in expected ranges

Bases: object

ammonyte.tests.test_utils_metrics module¶

Tests for ammonyte.utils.metrics Naming rules: 1. class: Test{filename}{Class}{method} with appropriate camel case 2. function: test_{method}_t{test_id}

Notes on how to test: 0. Make sure [pytest](https://docs.pytest.org) has been installed: pip install pytest 1. execute pytest {directory_path} in terminal to perform all tests in all testing files inside the specified directory 2. execute pytest {file_path} in terminal to perform all tests in the specified file 3. execute pytest {file_path}::{TestClass}::{test_method} in terminal to perform a specific test class/method inside the specified file 4. after pip install pytest-xdist, one may execute “pytest -n 4” to test in parallel with number of workers specified by -n 5. for more details, see https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/usage.html

class ammonyte.tests.test_utils_metrics.TestDetectionMetrics[source]¶

Bases: object

Tests for DetectionMetrics class and evaluate_detection function

test_attribute_access_t0()[source]¶

Test that all attributes are accessible

test_counts_consistency_t0()[source]¶

Test that TP + FP = n_detected and TP + FN = n_ground_truth

test_no_matches_t0()[source]¶

Test when no detections match ground truth

test_perfect_detection_t0()[source]¶

Test perfect detection scenario

test_return_type_t0()[source]¶

Test evaluate_detection returns DetectionMetrics object

test_to_dict_t0()[source]¶

Test to_dict method

(certain tests will only work when run from the tests directory, so make sure to run from there!)

  2. execute pytest {file_path}::{TestClass}::{test_method} in terminal to perform a specific test class/method inside the specified file

  3. after pip install pytest-xdist, one may execute “pytest -n 4” to test in parallel with number of workers specified by -n
